posts.author: Sabbath
posts.body: Predictions: 11 of 11 Pending | Tied for 1st with 964 others Diaz vs PerryThis is a rare fight announcement where I’m much more looking forward to the press conference than the ensuing bout of fisticuffs. Hearing a 42 year old Nate Diaz argue with the most Florida man who ever did Florida-ed is going to make my IQ score drop quicker than the stocks on Wall Street during Black Monday. They will exchange in a duel of words so incoherent that even the English speakers would need subtitles beforehand. As for the actual fight itself? Ehhhh, I dunno, flip a ******* coin. I personally prefer Mike win simply because Diaz fans are 95% 14 year old wannabe wiggers who think being a “gangster” is still a cool thing to be. It’s not. If there’s one thing I’ve learned from watching 85% of gangster movies, calling yourself a gangster IRL is the equivalent of calling yourself an inbred sociopath who would kill their mother for a dollar. The same people who say they love Scarface, which translated, really meant to say they love the third act of Scarface, and ignore pretty much everything else about it, including the weird incest ****, just to get their pickles tickled over the pre-********* teenage fantasy of being a sigma male. Losers, basically. Perry via R2 Face Punch

posts.author: Sabbath
posts.body: Predictions: 11 of 11 Pending | Tied for 1st with 964 others Santos vs DespaigneI don’t know about the rest of you, but I grow very tired of this silly silly idea of what kind of UFC fighter would transition well into high level boxing, or vice versa. Cause the answer is obvious: they would fail every single time. Why? Because the question isn’t based on skill, it’s based on the surroundings. You ask who wins between a tiger and a shark, the answer is always gonna be the following question: ‘Where does the fight take place?’If it’s in a pool of water, the tiger is as helpless as a newborn baby against a shark. On land, the shark dies before it even gets a chance to defend itself. That’s all MMA/Boxing crossovers are, an exercise in proving obvious **** that doesn’t need to be answered. In a somewhat related example, take Despaigne. Put him in a purely striking bout where kicks are involved, and he will run through an entire mob like a blood raged viking berserker on ketamine seeking vengeance for his recently deceased wife. Put him in an MMA fight with someone who can grapple, and now, he’s the tiger in the pool of water. Thankfully, his agent or matchmaker was smart enough to give him the perfect matchup - the shriveled up half-dead pseudo-corpse of Junior Dos Santos. However, despite Santos being older than phone booths, and having a body more prosthetic than flesh due to his wear & tear, he does have a slight chance of winning this, if he chooses to grapple. Of course, Santos has pretty much never been the type to go straight into lay & pray dry humping in his fights, but in this case it’s pretty much his only resort to actually winning this fight. And he could pull it off, realistically. Keep in mind, his opponent is the same guy who got out wrestled by Austin Lane, an utterly chinless meme of a jobber. ****, I could probably hold Despaigne down for fifteen minutes at this point, and the only blue belt I have is on this ******* website. Still, odds are very low for poor Santos. Fingers crossed he pulls it off. If not for a good feeling to see an old vet get one last win in, then at least for a good laugh at his opponent’s expense.Despaigne via R1 Elder Abuse * Edited at 03.26.2026, 6:54 AM ET *
